learning_outcomes:
CHEDN_Learning Outcomes_ENG 1.  |  Major
Programme:
Upon
completion of the Programme, graduates of the B.A. Programme in Early
Childhood Education will be able to:
1.  |  acquire knowledge on understanding the central concepts,
tools of inquiry, and structures of early childhood education;
2.  |  design curriculum and instructional plans based on children's diverse abilities;
3.  |  apply a variety of instructional strategies to encourage
the development of curiosity, creativity, problem-solving and performance
skills in children;
4.  |  acquire knowledge and skills on a variety of formal and
informal assessment strategies, and to use various assessment strategies to
evaluate and ensure the continuous all-round development of young learners;
5.  |  demonstrate an
understanding of the basic organizational and managerial theories, and ways
to adopt appropriate strategies to contribute, independently and collegially,
to the quality of their schools to the benefit of children, parents, the
community;
6.  |  recognize the complex characteristics of young children,
young children's families and communities, and to appreciate respectful and
reciprocal relationships that support children's development and learning; and
7.  |  develop themselves to be
innovative reflective practitioners who continually evaluate the effects
of their choices and who actively research and
implement new methods and seek out opportunities to grow
professionally.
study_scheme:
Early Childhood Education (2 years) Early Childhood Education Applicable to students admitted in 2023-24
Major Programme Requirement (for Higher Diploma holders
admitted to senior-year places)
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 55 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:
(a)  |  Educational
Studies
EDUC2120, 2220, 2240  |  8
(b)  |  Subject Knowledge
CHED3160, 4120, 4140, 4220[a], 4340[a],
4350[a], 4360, 4410, 4510, 4530  |  29
(c)  |  Research Experience
CHED4320[a], 4540[a]  |  7
(d)  |  Internship
CHED4550[a]  |  3
2.  |  Elective Courses:
8 units of courses chosen from the
following:
BECE3130#, CHED4150, 4210, 4310[a],
4330, 4370, 4520, EDUC2210, 3150, 3200, 3270, 3280, 3290, MUSC2545#, SPED3920  |  8
Total:  |  55
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  CHED, EDUC and SPED courses at 2000 and above level as
included in the Major Programme Requirement as well as those labeled as #
will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification.
[a]  |  Capstone courses
